title: Incidental mildly elevated liver enzymes

chart.cc: Abnormal liver tests on routine labs
chart.oneLiner: 48M with obesity and type 2 diabetes is referred for mildly elevated liver enzymes found on routine screening; he feels well.

opening: My doctor said my liver numbers were a bit high on a routine blood test. I feel fine, though.
patientFile:
PERSONA: 48-year-old man, asymptomatic, well-appearing, somewhat surprised. Good historian.

HPI: No symptoms — no jaundice, no abdominal pain, no dark urine. Mildly elevated transaminases found on routine labs. On asking: known obesity, type 2 diabetes, high cholesterol, and a sedentary lifestyle (metabolic syndrome). Minimal alcohol (quantify to exclude alcoholic liver disease). No new medications or supplements/herbals (verify). NO IV drug use, NO risky exposures, NO family history of liver disease.
PMH: type 2 diabetes, obesity, dyslipidemia. MEDS: metformin, atorvastatin (note: statins are generally safe to continue). SOCIAL: 1–2 drinks per week, nonsmoker.

PHYSICAL EXAM (report ONLY what is requested; give normals when asked):
- General: obese, well-appearing, no jaundice.
- Vitals: BP 136/86, HR 78, RR 14, 36.8°C, SpO2 99% RA.
- Abdomen: soft; mild hepatomegaly if examined; NO stigmata of chronic liver disease (no spider angiomata/ascites).

HIDDEN RULES:
- NEVER state or hint at a diagnosis. Answer only what is asked.
- Do NOT volunteer alcohol quantity, supplements, or metabolic history unless asked about alcohol, medications/supplements, or medical history.
- If asked something not in this file, give a reasonable normal/negative answer.

labs.LFTs: AST 48, ALT 70 (mild elevation, ALT>AST — typical of NAFLD), Alk Phos normal.
labs.Metabolic: A1c elevated, triglycerides high, LDL high — metabolic syndrome.
labs.OtherCauses: Iron studies, ceruloplasmin, autoimmune markers, and TSH sent to exclude hemochromatosis/Wilson/autoimmune/thyroid; unremarkable.
labs.US: RUQ ultrasound: increased echogenicity consistent with hepatic steatosis (fatty liver).
labs.Fibrosis: Non-invasive fibrosis assessment (FIB-4 / elastography) to stage.

ekg.label: 12-lead EKG
ekg.findings: Short PR, delta wave (slurred QRS upstroke), widened QRS.
ekg.verify: Source a study matching: Short PR, delta wave (slurred QRS upstroke), widened QRS. Self-host with appropriate credit; do not reuse the prior normal tracing.
ekg.attribution: LITFL ECG Library (litfl.com) — © Life in the Fast Lane, CC BY-NC-SA 4.0. Educational/not-for-profit use, credit litfl.com; skip any image labelled third-party.

cxr.label: Chest X-ray
cxr.findings: Thin apical pleural line with absent lung markings peripherally; small apical pneumothorax.
cxr.verify: Source a study matching: Thin apical pleural line with absent lung markings peripherally; small apical pneumothorax. Self-host with appropriate credit; do not reuse the prior normal tracing.
cxr.imageDescription: chest radiograph showing small apical pneumothorax

steps.label: Differential
steps.prompt: Based on the history and labs, list your differential for the mildly elevated transaminases (note your leading diagnosis).
steps.rubric: Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease / NASH (leading,7). Alcoholic liver disease (must quantify alcohol,4). Chronic viral hepatitis (CANT-MISS to exclude,4). Drug/supplement-induced (core,3). Hemochromatosis / Wilson / autoimmune hepatitis (core,2). Thyroid/celiac as secondary causes (bonus,2). Names NAFLD while committing to exclude other causes (core,3).
steps.idealAnswer: Leading diagnosis: Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease / NASH. Must-not-miss diagnoses to exclude: Chronic viral hepatitis. Also reasonable: Alcoholic liver disease; Drug/supplement-induced; Hemochromatosis / Wilson / autoimmune hepatitis.

coreActions.item: Names NAFLD while committing to exclude other causes

steps.label: Workup
steps.prompt: What initial workup do you want to order?
steps.rubric: Confirm/trend LFTs (2). Viral hepatitis serologies B and C (CANT-MISS,3). Metabolic workup — A1c, lipids (2). Iron studies, ceruloplasmin (if young), autoimmune markers, TSH to exclude other causes (3). RUQ ultrasound (2). Non-invasive fibrosis assessment — FIB-4 / elastography (2). PENALTY: diagnosing NAFLD without excluding other causes (-2).
steps.idealAnswer: Order: EKG; CXR; LFTs; Viral serologies; Metabolic; Other causes; US; Fibrosis.

criticalActions.item: Viral hepatitis serologies B and C

penalties.item: diagnosing NAFLD without excluding other causes

requiredTests: Viral hepatitis serologies B and C
contraindicatedTests: diagnosing NAFLD without excluding other causes

steps.label: Read EKG
steps.prompt: Here is the patient's 12-lead EKG. Describe your findings.
steps.rubric: Findings = Wolff-Parkinson-White pre-excitation (short PR, delta wave / slurred QRS upstroke, widened QRS). CREDIT: systematic rate & rhythm read (7); identifies the short PR and delta wave (5); recognizes the WPW pre-excitation pattern (3). PENALTY: calling the study normal / mistaking it for bundle branch block or missing the abnormality (-3).
commonMistakes: mistaking the delta-wave widening for bundle branch block or ventricular rhythm
steps.idealAnswer: WPW pre-excitation: short PR, delta wave, broad QRS.

penalties.label: calls the study normal / misses the abnormality

steps.label: Read CXR
steps.prompt: Here is the patient's chest X-ray. Describe your findings.
steps.rubric: Findings = small apical pneumothorax (thin visceral pleural line with absent lung markings peripherally). CREDIT: identifies the visceral pleural line with absent peripheral markings (6); recognizes a (small) pneumothorax (4). PENALTY: calling the study normal / mistaking a skin fold for the pleural line or missing the abnormality (-3).
keyFindings: visceral pleural line with absent peripheral markings
criticalFindings: Apical pneumothorax (pleural line, absent peripheral lung markings).
commonMistakes: mistaking a skin fold for the pleural line
steps.idealAnswer: Small apical pneumothorax: a thin visceral pleural line with no lung markings beyond it.

criticalActions: Apical pneumothorax (pleural line, absent peripheral lung markings).
coreActions: visceral pleural line with absent peripheral markings

penalties.label: calls the study normal / misses the abnormality

steps.label: Revised Dx
steps.prompt: Mild ALT>AST elevation, negative viral/other workup, steatosis on ultrasound, and metabolic syndrome. What is your diagnosis and next assessment?
steps.rubric: Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (likely NASH given diabetes) (6). Stages fibrosis with a non-invasive tool (FIB-4 / elastography) to identify those needing closer follow-up (4).
steps.idealAnswer: NAFLD/NASH (likely NASH given diabetes). Next, stage fibrosis non-invasively (FIB-4 or elastography) to identify patients needing closer follow-up/hepatology referral.

steps.label: Management
steps.rubric: Per MGH MASLD/NAFLD: lifestyle weight loss 7-10% – diet and exercise (CANT-MISS,7); manage metabolic comorbidities – DM/dyslipidemia/HTN, statins safe (5); alcohol avoidance (3); stage fibrosis (FIB-4) and refer if advanced (3); consider GLP-1/metabolic therapy + vaccinate (2). PENALTY: withholding a statin out of unfounded fear of liver injury (-2); no attention to metabolic risk factors (-2).
steps.idealAnswer: Lifestyle-based weight loss is first-line: 7–10% body-weight loss reduces steatohepatitis/fibrosis; diet and exercise. Aggressively manage metabolic comorbidities – diabetes, dyslipidemia, hypertension; statins are safe. Avoid alcohol. Stage fibrosis (FIB-4) and refer to hepatology if advanced. Consider GLP-1 agonist/metabolic therapy and vaccinate (HAV/HBV).

coreActions.item: Stage fibrosis (FIB-4) and refer if advanced

optionalActions: Stage fibrosis (FIB-4) and refer if advanced

Use lay language; avoid medical jargon and textbook phrasing

physicalExamMappings.finding: Abdomen: soft; mild hepatomegaly if examined; NO stigmata of chronic liver disease (no spider angiomata/ascites).

caseSummary.diagnosticFramework: 48M with obesity and type 2 diabetes is referred for mildly elevated liver enzymes found on routine screening; he feels well. The diagnosis is NAFLD / NASH, reached by eliciting the focused history, performing the targeted exam, and interpreting the directed workup while excluding the must-not-miss alternatives.
keyPhysicalExamFindings: Abdomen: soft; mild hepatomegaly if examined; NO stigmata of chronic liver disease (no spider angiomata/ascites).
reasoningPathway: Frame the presentation from the chart and vitals.
Elicit the focused history, including sensitive items only on direct questioning.
Perform the targeted physical exam maneuvers to reveal key findings.
Build a differential led by NAFLD / NASH while explicitly excluding must-not-miss causes.
Order a focused, order-sensitive workup and interpret EKG/CXR and labs.
Commit to NAFLD / NASH and manage with stabilization, definitive treatment, consults, and disposition.
criticalWorkupItems: Viral hepatitis serologies B and C
criticalInterpretationItems: Apical pneumothorax (pleural line, absent peripheral lung markings).
keyImageInterpretationFindings: Apical pneumothorax (pleural line, absent peripheral lung markings).
criticalImageInterpretationFindings: Apical pneumothorax (pleural line, absent peripheral lung markings).
imageInterpretationPearls: An incidental abnormality is an interpretation task in its own right — name it, then judge its relevance.
Read every study systematically before committing.
State pertinent negatives, not just positives.

items.item: Shows empathy for the patient's concern
Explains next steps in patient-friendly language
